explicit_text: I was born in December 1906 at Jasper, Florida, and lived there until I was twenty-two years old. My family, including my brother Green Johnson and sister Fiat Johnson, were all born there as well. My parents, Sam and Carrie Johnson, were sharecroppers near Jasper, and I was brought up on the farm doing hard work like hoeing and picking cotton. I even got married there the first time, to Thomas Roux.
implicit_text: I was born in December 1906 in a small town in northern Florida, and lived there until I was twenty-two years old. My family, including my brother Green Johnson and sister Fiat Johnson, were all born there as well. My parents, Sam and Carrie Johnson, were sharecroppers near that town, and I was brought up on the farm doing hard work like hoeing and picking cotton. I even got married there the first time, to Thomas Roux.

explicit_text: I moved to Lakeland, Florida, in December 1919 after leaving the farm near Jasper. In Lakeland, my family and I have lived and worked; for example, my son Mark works as a caretaker for the Oak Hill Burial Park there. I also worked out in service when I first came to Lakeland, and my children have attended school there. Lakeland has been home to us for many years.
implicit_text: I moved to a city in central Florida in December 1919 after leaving the farm near my hometown. In this city, my family and I have lived and worked; for example, my son Mark works as a caretaker for a local burial park there. I also worked out in service when I first came to this city, and my children have attended school there. This place has been home to us for many years.

explicit_text: At one point, I lived in Manatee County, Florida, where I worked in the celery fields picking celery and earned up to twelve dollars and fifty cents a week. My first husband, Thomas Roux, was last known to be living in Manatee. It was a place where I experienced hard work and tough times before moving to Lakeland.
implicit_text: At one point, I lived in a county on Florida's Gulf Coast, where I worked in the celery fields picking celery and earned up to twelve dollars and fifty cents a week. My first husband, Thomas Roux, was last known to be living in that area. It was a place where I experienced hard work and tough times before moving to the city in central Florida.

explicit_text: My son W.J. once surprised us all when he answered a question about who made the first flag in the United States by saying George Washington. It was a memorable moment because he had been slipping away from Scout meetings and causing some trouble, but his knowledge impressed everyone. George Washington is a name that came up in our family stories and schooling.
implicit_text: My son W.J. once surprised us all when he answered a question about who made the first flag in the United States by saying the first president of the country. It was a memorable moment because he had been slipping away from Scout meetings and causing some trouble, but his knowledge impressed everyone. That founding father is a name that came up in our family stories and schooling.

explicit_text: My son Olen is involved with the National Youth Administration, or NYA, and he receives a check every two weeks for five dollars or more. He also secures odd jobs when off duty and helps contribute to our household expenses like rent and food. The NYA has been an important support for him and for us during these times.
implicit_text: My son Olen is involved with a federal program aimed at helping young people, and he receives a check every two weeks for five dollars or more. He also secures odd jobs when off duty and helps contribute to our household expenses like rent and food. This youth employment program has been an important support for him and for us during these times.

explicit_text: Olen also has to go to the Works Progress Administration office to see the YA supervisor, which shows how connected he is with government programs helping young people. The WPA has been part of his life as he tries to find work and support himself and the family. These programs have been crucial during the hard times we've faced.
implicit_text: Olen also has to go to a government office that oversees public works projects to see the youth program supervisor, which shows how connected he is with government programs helping young people. This federal agency has been part of his life as he tries to find work and support himself and the family. These programs have been crucial during the hard times we've faced.

explicit_text: My son Mark works for the city of Lakeland as the caretaker for the Oak Hill Burial Park. He earns two dollars and fifty cents a day doing this job, which is sometimes irregular but steady enough to help our family. Oak Hill Burial Park is a place connected to our daily lives through Mark's work.
implicit_text: My son Mark works for the city of Lakeland as the caretaker for a local burial park. He earns two dollars and fifty cents a day doing this job, which is sometimes irregular but steady enough to help our family. This burial park is a place connected to our daily lives through Mark's work.

explicit_text: When I was sick with a sore on my leg that started about six years ago, I tried many treatments without success until a root worker connected me with Madame Jackson. She is a palmister from Alabama who sends me treatment every week, and I believe it is helping me heal slowly. Madame Jackson's care has been a source of hope for me when I had tried everything else.
